channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Graham says Republicans may 'own another attack' on US if Trump border czar doesn't get deportation funds fast
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., said congressional Republicans who delay funding for border czar Tom Homan could "own another attack on our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: There will be a lot on the plate for new Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth when he steps into his office on the Pentagon’s third floor E Ring.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of Ohio, his home state.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's anti-DEI push puts top colleges in the crosshairs
item.description: Universities now find themselves at the frontlines of President Trump's war against diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs. An executive order this week instructed federal institutions to investigate DEI programs at schools with endowments of more than $1 billion endowments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s bid to end birthright citizenship sparks GOP divisions
item.description: President Trump’s bid to end birthright citizenship is dividing House Republicans, highlighting internal differences on the thorny topic of immigration just as the GOP is making gains with Hispanic voters.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: ICE arrests 3 Tren de Aragua gang members in mass deportation push
item.description: Three Venezuelan gang members in the country illegally were arrested by ICE this week as President Donald Trump ramps up his promised mass deportations his first week in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hegseth is quickly sworn in as defense secretary after dramatic Senate vote
item.description: Pete Hegseth was sworn in Saturday as the nation's 29th secretary of defense, quickly joining President Trump's Cabinet after a dramatic late-night vote in the Senate installed him as the Pentagon's leader.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's DOJ asks Supreme Court to freeze cases on student debt, environment
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department on Friday asked the Supreme Court to freeze four pending cases implicating the environment or student debt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Bessent nomination to lead Treasury
item.description: The Senate on Saturday voted to advance Scott Bessent’s nomination to become the next secretary of the Treasury, putting him in line to be confirmed on Monday. Senators voted 67-23 to tee up final passage on Bessent’s nomination. The final vote will take place Monday evening at 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: He's emboldened, he's organized and he's still Trump: Takeaways from the president's opening days
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s first week in office isn't over yet, but already it offers signals about how his next four years in the White House may unfold.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How key Republican senators are responding to Trump allies' primary threats
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — From activists on the far right to Elon Musk, allies of President Donald Trump have been quick to threaten primary challengers for Republican senators who don't back his Cabinet picks and legislative priorities.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This is how Trump organized his Oval Office decor
item.description: Every president gets to decorate the Oval Office to their liking — but sometimes, they keep the decor of their predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Alaska Sen. Murkowski has faced Trump's wrath. She was willing to defy him after his return to power
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — In the early days of President Donald Trump's second term, Alaska Sen. Lisa Murkowski has openly challenged or rebuked him at least three times — stunning for a congressional Republican who has faced his wrath before and yet remains unbowed by pressure to embrace his agenda.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's 'two sexes' executive order comes on heels of SCOTUS accepting another challenge to LGBT agenda
item.description: In his first week in office, President Donald Trump has enshrined only two genders, male and female, in federal policy through one of his slew of executive actions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate forces rare weekend vote to push through Kristi Noem as Homeland Security chief
item.description: The Senate will meet for a rare session on Saturday to vote on whether to confirm Gov. Kristi Noem as the director of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservatives of color have lofty expectations for Trump's second term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Delivering his first address as a reinaugurated president, Donald Trump spoke directly to communities that had historically shunned his party.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kern County reacts: Trump proposes voter ID mandate for California elections
item.description: Back when 17 News covered that story, we asked viewers if they thought voter ID should be required. More than 700 voted, and 92% said voter ID should be checked at the polls.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Defense Department orders 'pause' of social media accounts
item.description: The Defense Department said it needs time to review its social media programming to match Trump's priorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Vance's tiebreaker vote confirms Pete Hegseth as Defense secretary
item.description: Vance cast the tiebreaker vote after Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., voted no for Hegseth, leaving the chamber at a 50-50 tie.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk tried to turn the salute controversy into a joke
item.description: The incident only lasted seconds, but it sparked what has become a global debate about how to interpret what Musk did. Then Musk started posting Nazi-themed jokes.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Top Minnesota attorney: Birthright citizenship suit against Trump just the beginning
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship two days after Minnesota joined 21 other states in a lawsuit to challenge it. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison discussed his strategy.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Lisa Murkowski to vote against Hegseth; first Republican to oppose a Trump Cabinet pick
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski announced Thursday that she will vote against confirming Pete Hegseth to lead Pentagon, becoming the first Republican to oppose one of President Donald Trump's Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stick with us: Why the internet is obsessed with one of the simplest tools known to man
item.description: "Stick Nation" is a worldwide movement that thrives on irreverent, fantastical and downright wholesome descriptions of sticks.
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian, MPR News

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: House Republicans seek to roll back DFL taxes, fees tied to transportation programs
item.description: Minnesota House Republicans are seeking to roll back taxes and fees approved when Democrats had full control of state government. A delivery fee and automatic increases to the gas taxes are on the GOP chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada, 17 other states sue Trump administration over birthright citizenship order
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states, including Nevada, sued the Trump administration Tuesday to stop it from seeking to eliminate birthright citizenship, a protection guaranteed in the U.S. Constitution.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5
